armored_hulk.name: Armored Hulk
description: ## Armored Hulk
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/28›

Some barbarians disdain the hides and leather used as armor by most of their kin. Instead they master the heaviest of armors, even those created by more civilized people, to gain greater protection and stability in battle.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power ability="An armored hulk gains proficiency in heavy armor."}

::ab[Indomitable Stance (Ex)]{icon=boost-def passive="An armored hulk gains a +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks and to CMD for overrun combat maneuvers, and on Reflex saves against ‹umr/trample› attacks. She also gains a +1 bonus to her AC against charge attacks and on attack and damage rolls against charging creatures." replace="Fast movement"}
::ab[Armored Swiftness (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="When wearing medium or heavy armor, an armored hulk can move 5 feet faster than normal, to a maximum of her speed." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Resilience of Steel (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="While wearing heavy armor, she gains a bonus to AC that applies only on critical hit confirmation rolls. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Improved Armored Swiftness (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="An armored hulk's land speed is faster than the norm for her race by +10 feet." special="This benefit applies when she is wearing any armor, including heavy armor, but not while carrying a heavy load. Apply this bonus before modifying the armored hulk's speed because of any load carried or armor worn. This bonus stacks with any other bonuses to the barbarian's land speed." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the armored hulk archetype: ‹ragepower/boasting taunt›, ‹ragepower/greater guarded life›, ‹ragepower/guarded life›, ‹ragepower/guarded stance›, ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/no escape›, ‹ragepower/overbearing advance›, ‹ragepower/overbearing onslaught›, ‹ragepower/reflexive dodge›, ‹ragepower/rolling dodge›, ‹ragepower/unexpected strike›.

beastkin_berserker.name: Beastkin Berserker
description: ## Beastkin Berserker
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Darklands/21›

While some barbarians take on bestial aspects in their rages, the beastkin berserker descends so deeply into primal fury that she actually transforms into an animal. Berserkers of the surface world often associate with predators such as bears or wolves, and are sometimes mistaken for lycanthropes. The barbarians in Deep Tolguth shapechange into rampaging dinosaurs or megafauna, crafting their armor out of the hide and bones of their favored animals.

::ab[Savage Rapport (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A beastkin berserker gains a spiritual connection to a specific kind of creature with the animal type (such as a ‹monster/badger› or a ‹monster/triceratops›). She adds ‹misc/half› her level (minimum 1) on Handle Animal, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks involving the chosen type of animal." imp5="The beastkin berserker can select a second kind of animal to which these bonuses apply." imp10="The beastkin berserker can select a third kind of animal to which these bonuses apply." imp15="The beastkin berserker can select a fourth kind of animal to which these bonuses apply." imp20="The beastkin berserker can select a fifth kind of animal to which these bonuses apply." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Feral Transformation (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="When entering a rage, a beastkin berserker can take the form of a Small or Medium animal. This functions as ‹spell/beast shape I›, except the duration is for as long as the beastkin berserker rages and the animal must be one she has chosen with her *savage rapport.*" imp8="The beastkin berserker can use *feral transformation* to take the form of a Large or Tiny animal that she has chosen with her savage rapport. *Feral transformation* now acts as ‹spell/beast shape II›." imp12="A beastkin berserker is able to use *feral transformation* to take the form of a Huge or Diminutive animal that she has chosen with her savage rapport. *Feral transformation* now functions as ‹spell/beast shape III›." replace="4th, 8th, and 12th level rage powers"}

breaker.name: Breaker
description: ## Breaker
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/78›

While most barbarians are skilled at breaking things, some find the need to destroy their surroundings an almost uncontrollable urge when in the middle of a rage. These barbarians are a danger not only to their foes, but also to the very environment around them. A breaker barbarian has the following class features.

::ab[Destructive (Ex)]{icon=melee-boost passive="Whenever the breaker barbarian makes a melee attack that targets an unattended object or makes a ‹rule/sunder› combat maneuver, she adds ‹misc/half› her barbarian level (minimum +1) on the damage roll." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Battle Scavenger (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="The breaker barbarian suffers no penalty on attack rolls when using an ‹misc/improvised weapon› or a weapon with the ‹misc/broken› condition." imp6="She gains a +1 bonus on damage rolls with improvised or broken weapons." increment="This bonus~6~3" replace="Trap sense"}
@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the breaker archetype: ‹ragepower/ground breaker›, ‹ragepower/smasher›, ‹ragepower/strength surge›.

brutal_pugilist.name: Brutal Pugilist
description: ## Brutal Pugilist
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/78›

Some barbarians focus on using their bare hands to tear their opponents limb from limb. These brutal pugilists also learn a great deal about various ‹rule/combat maneuvers›, using them to cripple or crush their foes. A brutal pugilist has the following class features.

::ab[Savage Grapple (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-melee order=pa passive="The brutal pugilist takes only ‹misc/half› the normal penalties to Dexterity, attack rolls, and combat maneuver checks when she has the ‹rule/grapple«d» condition<s›." ability="She can make an attack of opportunity against creatures trying to grapple her even if they possess the ‹feat/Improved Grapple› feat or the ‹umr/grab› special attack. If she hits with this attack of opportunity, she gains a +2 circumstance bonus to her CMD against the grapple attempt. She cannot make these attacks of opportunity once a grapple has succeeded." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Pit Fighter (Ex)]{l=3 icon=melee-def flavor="The brutal pugilist has learned combat tricks from fighting in pit brawls and gladiatorial arenas." ability="She selects one ‹rule/combat maneuver<s› and gains a +1 insight bonus on her CMB or to her CMD in that maneuver. This bonus increases to +2 if the barbarian is wearing no armor (shields are allowed)." special="At every three levels after 3rd (6th, 9th 12th, 15th, and 18th), the barbarian may select another combat maneuver and add this bonus on her CMB or to her CMD. This bonus can be applied to each maneuver no more than twice, once on CMB and once to CMD." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Improved Savage Grapple (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="The brutal pugilist takes no penalties to Dexterity, attack rolls, and combat maneuver checks when she has the grappled condition. She also is treated as one size larger than her actual size when determining whether she can be grappled using the ‹umr/grab› ability or ‹umr/swallow«ed<_whole› by another creature." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the brutal pugilist archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/brawler›, ‹ragepower/greater brawler›, ‹ragepower/knockback›, ‹ragepower/knockdown›, ‹ragepower/overbearing advance›, ‹ragepower/overbearing onslaught›, ‹ragepower/strength surge›.

brutish_swamper.name: Brutish Swamper
description: ## Brutish Swamper
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/37›

The hardy and insular denizens of the swamp produce warriors of narrow focus and great strength. The prejudice and misunderstanding with which civilized folk often regard barbarians is often even more pronounced in the case of these belligerent outcasts from society, in large part due to the fact that brutish swampers deliberately mock and antagonize the so-called "city folk." To a brutish swamper, an urban lifestyle is one that breeds weakness and lassitude in the body. The brutish swamper has little interest or patience in maintaining alliances or friendship with those who live such soft lifestyles, and this aggression only further encourages feelings of prejudice on both sides of the cultural divide. When a brutish swamper joins an adventuring party, they usually come to value the skills and talents of their more urbanized companions, but never fully understand the desire to live lives surrounded by creature comforts.
::cskill{gain=kg~kl}

::ab[Marsh March (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A brutish swamper can move through the underbrush and muddy terrain of bogs and swamps at her normal speed without taking damage or suffering other impairments. Briars, mud, thorns, and overgrown areas that have been magically manipulated to impede motion, however, still affect her." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Home (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A brutish swamper gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks when in swamps." imp5="The bonus becomes +3." imp8="The bonus becomes +4." imp11="The bonus becomes +5." imp14="The bonus becomes +6." imp17="The bonus becomes +7." imp20="The bonus becomes +8." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Stubborn (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A brutish swamper resists any attempts to change her attitude or compel her to action. Diplomacy and Intimidate checks against her take a -2 penalty, and no result can shift her attitude more than one step in a 24-hour period." increment="The penalty~3~3~-4~-2" replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Wrastlin (Ex)]{l=6 icon=def-melee passive="While raging, a brutish swamper gains a +2 bonus to her CMD against ‹rule/grapple› combat maneuvers and a +2 bonus on damage rolls made as part of successful grapple combat maneuver checks." replace="6th-level rage power"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the brutish swamper archetype: ‹ragepower/brawler›, ‹ragepower/greater brawler›, ‹ragepower/greater tyrant totem›, ‹ragepower/lesser tyrant totem›, ‹ragepower/lizard stride›, ‹ragepower/low-light vision›, ‹ragepower/superstition›, ‹ragepower/tyrant totem›.

cave_dweller.name: Cave Dweller
description: ## Cave Dweller
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/37›

Unused to the light of the sun, cave dwellers use their subterranean expertise to protect their clans from the manifold dangers of deep caverns and tunnels. The typical cave dweller is perfectly content to live his entire life underground, and he may even view the light of the sun as particularly abhorrent or taboo. While this lifestyle doesn't in and of itself imply evil or sinister intent, many outlanders ignorantly assume that cave dwellers avoid the light of the sun for malicious reasons. In truth, cave dwellers are as capable of goodness and kindness as any other - they just see little reason to venture out of their preferred homelands. When a cave dweller joins an adventuring party, she may retain an inherent nervousness about sleeping under the open sky and may long for the familiar confines of a comforting cavern, but this rarely translates directly into a notable disadvantage. Indeed, many adventuring groups come to value the specialized talents and skills the cave dweller brings to the party.
::cskill{gain=stl lose=ride}

::ab[Tight Tunnels (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="Cave dwellers pursue prey through the winding tunnels of the deep, which rarely accommodate open sprints. A cave dweller can make a single turn up to 90 degrees while ‹rule/run«ning› (though not while ‹rule/charging›). Additionally, a cave dweller takes no penalties to movement speed while ‹misc/squeezing›." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Tunnel Vision (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost passive="A cave dweller gains darkvision to a range of 60 feet and a bonus on Perception checks to detect ambushes, movement, and sound while in darkness. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-third› her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Sun Walker (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def passive="A cave dweller gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC and on saving throws against effects with the light descriptor or that produce bright or sudden light. Any penalties on attack rolls, saving throws, or skill checks because of bright light are reduced by 1." imp10="The bonus becomes +2 and the reduction is now 2." imp13="The bonus becomes +3 and the reduction is now 3." imp16="The bonus becomes +4 and the reduction is now 4." imp19="The bonus becomes +5 and the reduction is now 5." replace="Damage reduction"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the cave dweller archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/bestial climber›, ‹ragepower/eclipsing rage›, ‹ragepower/greater eclipsing rage›, ‹ragepower/greater ground breaker›, ‹ragepower/ground breaker›, ‹ragepower/night vision›, ‹ragepower/raging climber›, ‹ragepower/primal scent›, ‹ragepower/sprint›.

deepwater_rager.name: Deepwater Rager
description: ## Deepwater Rager
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/28›
Deepwater ragers bellow ferociously as they charge their enemies, tackling them into the raging seas. This archetype is compatible with both barbarians and unchained barbarians.

::ab[Strong Lungs (Ex)]{icon=power-boost passive="A deepwater rager can ‹misc/hold her breath› for a number of rounds equal to four times her Constitution score, and she does not reduce the duration of her held breath by taking a standard action or a full-round action. Additionally, as long as she is able to speak, the deepwater rager adds her Constitution modifier to Intimidate skill checks in addition to her Charisma modifier." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Spiraling Charge (Ex)]{l=2 icon=melee ability="A deepwater rager is not required to move in a straight line when she charges, provided that no space she moves into while charging is farther away from her target than the space that she is leaving. The deepwater rager must be able to see her target at the beginning of her charge and cannot use this ability while mounted." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Disorienting Grapple (Ex)]{l=5 icon=melee flavor="A deepwater rager becomes an expert in flipping and spinning opponents as they are grappled." passive="An opponent ‹rule/grapple«d› by the deepwater rager gains the ‹misc/sickened› condition for as long as it has the grappled condition. When the creature's grappled condition ends, for any reason, it must succeed at an Acrobatics check (with a DC equal to the deepwater rager's CMD) or fall ‹rule/prone›." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Crushing Grapple (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power-melee ability="A deepwater rager gains the ‹umr/constrict› special attack, which deals an amount of damage equal to the damage her unarmed strike deals." replace="Indomitable will"}
::ab[Full Lungs (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power passive="A deepwater rager does not need to breathe while she is raging." replace="Tireless rage"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the deepwater rager archetype: ‹ragepower/battle roar›, ‹ragepower/bestial swimmer›, ‹ragepower/brawler›, ‹ragepower/intimidating glare›, ‹ragepower/raging swimmer›, ‹ragepower/rolling dodge›, ‹ragepower/terrifying howl›.

dreadnought.name: Dreadnought
description: ## Dreadnought
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/47›
Unstoppable and fearless, the dreadnought turns the terror of her enemies into violent physical power.

::ab[Dead Calm (Ex)]{icon=power-down free="A dreadnought can enter a dispassionate killing spree, granting her additional combat prowess. The dreadnought gains only ‹misc/half› the usual bonuses from her rage but takes no penalty to her AC, can use all her normal skills and effects that require concentration, and is not fatigued when her rage ends." special="The dreadnought cannot enter a rage for 1 minute after she ends her rage, and she cannot charge or run while under the effects of her rage." alter=Rage}

::ab[Stead Gait (Ex)]{icon=def passive="A dreadnought advances steadily, shrugging off attempts to slow her advance. The dreadnought gains a +1 bonus on saving throws and to her CMD against effects that would slow or immobilize her." special="This bonus increases to +2 when she gains *greater rage* and +3 when she gains *mighty rage.*" replace="Fast movement"}
::ab[Fearless Killer (Su)]{l=14 flavor="A dreadnought becomes implacable in her pursuit of slaughter." icon=def passive="While in rage, she is immune to fear effects." replace="Indomitable will"}

::ab[Instant Dispassion (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost free="A dreadnought can quickly turn her compassion on and off. She can enter a rage as soon as she wants after ending a previous rage." replace="Tireless rage"}

drunken_brute.name: Drunken Brute
description: ## Drunken Brute
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/78›

Barbarians are known for their ability to consume potent drink, but drunken brutes turn drinking into a combat tactic, using the potent liquor to fuel their rage and grant them additional powers. A drunken brute has the following class feature.

::ab[Raging Drunk (Ex)]{icon=power move="While raging, the drunken brute can drink a potion, or a tankard of ale or similar quantity of alcohol, as a move action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity. A potion has its normal effect, while an alcoholic drink allows the barbarian to maintain her rage that round without expending a round of rage for the day (instead of the alcohol's normal effects)." special="For each alcoholic drink consumed while raging, the barbarian is ‹misc/nauseated› for 1 round when her rage expires, in addition to the normal fatigue that follows a rage. Tireless rage does not negate this nauseated condition but the *internal fortitude* rage power does." replace="Fast movement"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the drunken brute archetype: ‹ragepower/boasting taunt›, ‹ragepower/good for what ails you›, ‹ragepower/internal fortitude›, ‹ragepower/liquid courage›, ‹ragepower/moment of clarity›, ‹ragepower/roaring drunk›, ‹ragepower/staggering drunk›.

drunken_rager.name: Drunken Rager
description: ## Drunken Rager
‹SOURCE Alchemy Manual/12›
These hotheaded, hard-drinking ruffians and brawlers are as dangerous in the midst of combat as they are in a tavern, and they wouldn't be caught dead without a libation for either occasion.

::ab[Drunken Rage (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power order=sf standard="A drunken rager can drink a unit of ale or other strong alcohol while raging to gain 1 drunken rage point. The act of drinking is a standard action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity." special="At 1st level, the barbarian can have a maximum of 1 drunken rage point; this maximum increases by 1 every 2 levels thereafter. These drunken rage points last for 1 hour or until spent, whichever comes first. The barbarian can use drunken rage points only while raging. Unless otherwise noted, spending a drunken rage point is a free action." free="A drunken rager can spend 1 drunken rage point to increase her movement speed by 20 feet for 1 round, provided she is wearing no armor, light armor, or medium armor and is not carrying a heavy load. She can also spend 1 drunken rage point to gain 1 additional round of her rage class feature, which lasts for 1 hour or until used." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Staggering Evasion (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="As long as she has at least 1 drunken rage point, a drunken rager gains ‹ability/evasion›, as the ‹class/rogue› class feature of the same name." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Tolerance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="As long as she has at least 1 drunken rage point, a drunken rager gains a bonus on saves against effects that would nauseate, poison, or sicken her, as well as saves to avoid ‹rule/addiction› or other ill effects associated with consuming alcohol. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Improved Staggering Evasion (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="As long as she has at least 2 drunken rage points, a drunken rager gains ‹talent/improved evasion›, as the advanced rogue talent of the same name." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Drunken Swing (Ex)]{l=12 icon=melee-boost swift="A drunken rager can spend 1 drunken rage point before making a melee attack to drastically increase the deadliness of her attack. The critical threat range of her next attack that round increases by 1 (for example, a critical threat range of 20 would increase to 19-20)." special="This ability does not stack with any other effect that expands the critical threat range of a weapon." imp16="She can instead spend 2 drunken rage points to increase the critical threat range of her next attack by 2." imp20="She can instead spend 3 drunken rage points to increase the critical threat range of her next attack by 3." replace="12th-level rage power"}

elemental_kin.name: Elemental Kin
description: ## Elemental Kin
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/78›

Some barbarian tribes have strong ties to the elemental forces of nature. Their shamans anoint the warriors at birth, tying them to the patron element of the tribe and granting them lasting boons against such forces. An elemental kin has the following class feature.

::ab[Elemental Fury (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="Whenever the elemental kin takes an amount of energy damage equal to or greater than her barbarian level while raging, she adds ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level to the total number of rounds that she can rage that day (1 extra round at 3rd level, 2 rounds at 6th level, and so on)." replace="Trap sense"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the elemental kin archetype: ‹ragepower/elemental rage›, ‹ragepower/energy absorption›, ‹ragepower/energy eruption›, ‹ragepower/energy resistance›, ‹ragepower/greater elemental rage›, ‹ragepower/greater energy resistance›, ‹ragepower/lesser elemental rage›.

fearsome_defender.name: Fearsome Defender
description: ## Fearsome Defender
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/47›

Dark overlords frequently recruit their most bloodthirsty followers to serve as brutal bodyguards. These savage slaves feel little pain and exist only to rain terror down on the enemies of their pitiless masters.

::ab[Intractable (Ex)]{icon=def flavor="A combination of brainwashing and loyalty helps a fearsome defender resist pain and torture, enduring grueling combat or questioning without betraying her dark lord." passive="A fearsome defender gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against pain effects, and the DCs of checks to coerce her or demoralize her with Diplomacy or Intimidate increase by 2." special="Both the bonus and the increase to DCs increase by 2 when she gains *greater rage* and again by 2 when she gains *mighty rage.*" replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Off the Leash (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power flavor="A fearsome defender simmers on the edge of bloody combat." ability="Whenever she begins a rage, she can also draw an unconcealed weapon as part of the free action to begin raging." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Silent Threat (Ex)]{l=3 flavor="A fearsome defender exudes hostility, creating a threatening atmosphere in her presence." icon=boost passive="The fearsome defender gains a +1 bonus on Intimidate checks, and the DCs of Intimidate checks to demoralize her allies within 30 feet increase by 1." special="Both the bonus and the increase to DCs increase by 1 at 6th level and every 3 barbarian levels thereafter." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Bloodlust (Ex)]{l=5 flavor="A fearsome defender's eagerness for bloodshed makes her quicker to act in a fight." icon=power passive="She adds her Charisma modifier to initiative checks and always acts during the surprise round. If the fearsome defender is surprised during the surprise round, she acts last in that round and then determines initiative order normally for the remainder of combat." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

feral_gnasher.name: Feral Gnasher
description: ## Feral Gnasher
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/116›

Feral gnashers grow up in the wild, either raised by animals or scraping by on their own, and soon learn to fend for themselves. These barbarians often utilize pieced-together armor and fight with their sharp teeth and whatever improvised weapons are within reach. A feral gnasher has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Goblin}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down passive="A feral gnasher loses all martial weapon proficiencies except for ‹eq-weapon/greatclub› and loses proficiency with medium armor."}

::ab[Savage Bite (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-melee ability="A feral gnasher gains a savage bite attack. This is a primary ‹umr/natural attack› that deals 1d4 points of damage. If the goblin already has the *hard head, big teeth* racial trait, the damage increases to 1d6." imp10="The damage from a feral gnasher's bite increases to 1d6 (or 1d8 if the goblin has the hard head, big teeth racial trait) and deals &times&3 damage on a critical hit." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Impromptu Armament (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A feral gnasher gains ‹feat/Throw Anything› as a bonus feat and can pick up an unattended object that can be wielded in one hand as a @HLfree_action. Additionally, the feral gnasher can take ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard› in place of a rage power." replace="2nd-level rage power"}

::ab[Lockjaw (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-melee ability="A feral gnasher gains the ‹umr/grab› ability with her bite attack. A feral gnasher can use this ability on a creature up to one size category larger than she is." replace="Trap sense +1"}
::ab[Improvised Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A feral gnasher gains ‹feat/Improvised Weapon Mastery› as a bonus feat." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Improved Lockjaw (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost-melee passive="As long as a feral gnasher is controlling the grapple with her *lockjaw* attack, she does not gain the grappled condition, but is unable to move or use her mouth for anything other than grappling." replace="Trap sense +2"}

::ab[Greater Lockjaw (Ex)]{icon=boost l9="A feral gnasher is able to use her *lockjaw's* grab ability on creatures two size increments larger than her." l15="A feral gnasher is able to use her *lockjaw's* grab ability on creatures three size increments larger than her." replace="Trap sense +3~trap sense +5"}

::ab[Wicked Improvisation (Ex)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="The feral gnasher gains a +1 competence bonus on damage rolls when using natural attacks or improvised weapons while raging." increment="The bonus~12~2" special="This increase is not ‹misc/precision damage› and is thus multiplied on a critical hit." replace="Trap sense +4"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the feral gnasher archetype: ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/scent›, ‹ragepower/superstition›, ‹ragepower/beast totem›, ‹ragepower/greater beast totem›, ‹ragepower/lesser beast totem›, ‹ragepower/eater of magic›, ‹ragepower/ghost rager›.

flesheater.name: Flesheater
description: ## Flesheater
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/114›
A flesheater eats flesh to create a spiritual bond between herself and the consumed creature, allowing her to take on aspects of the creature that served as the meal.

::ab[Rage]{icon=down passive="In additional to the standard bonuses and penalties of rage, a flesheater takes a -2 penalty to Intelligence while raging. The additional benefits and penalties from a flesheater's abilities last only as long as her rage does, and she cannot use these abilities if they would bring the total penalty to Intelligence to an amount equal to or greater than her Intelligence score." alter="The rage ability"}

::ab[One Flesh (Su)]{l=2 icon=power ability="When entering a rage, a flesheater can spend an additional round of rage and take an additional -2 penalty to Intelligence to take on a single ability of the valid consumed creature whose flesh she ate most recently. For the purpose of this ability, a valid consumed creature is any dead animal, dragon, humanoid, or magical beast that died and was consumed by the flesheater within the past 24 hours. She can choose any ability listed under ‹spell/beast shape I›, as long as the creature possessed that ability." imp5="The flesheater can take on any number of the creature's abilities that are listed under *beast shape I.*" imp8="The flesheater can instead take on one of the creature's abilities listed under ‹spell/beast shape II›. The flesheater's appearance alters to resemble the consumed creature, but she does not change shape. This is a polymorph effect." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge~the 2nd- and 8th-level rage powers"}

::ab[Unbound Rage (Su)]{l=11 flavor="A flesheater can draw in mass from the Ethereal Plane to enlarge herself when she enters a rage, as long as the creature whose flesh she last consumed was at least one size category larger than her base size." icon=power ability="Unbound rage affects her as ‹spell/enlarge person›, except she receives a +4 size bonus to Strength and an additional -2 penalty to Intelligence." replace="Greater rage"}

::ab[Feast (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost ability="A flesheater can use her *one flesh* and *unbound rage* abilities to emulate any one valid consumed creature of her choice, rather than only the one she consumed most recently." replace="The 14th-level rage power"}

::ab[Unbound Form (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="A flesheater can use her *one flesh* ability to assume the shape of a valid consumed creature. This ability functions as ‹spell/alter self›, ‹spell/beast shape IV›, ‹spell/form of the dragon III›, or ‹spell/giant form II›, as appropriate. This is a polymorph effect." replace="Mighty rage"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the flesheater archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/beast totem›, ‹ragepower/inspire ferocity›, ‹ragepower/internal fortitude›, and ‹ragepower/terrifying howl›.

geminate_invoker.name: Geminate Invoker
description: ## Geminate Invoker
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/166›

Geminate invokers are barbarians who use ‹rule/Rivethun› traditions to invite spirits into their bodies. The spirits' aid enables them to overcome the distractions of the flesh: they can ignore pain, and even defy death. These ascetics make up much of the Rivethun martial forces.

::ab[Contemplative (Ex)]{icon=skill-info order=pn passive="A geminate invoker adds ‹skill/Knowledge (history)›, ‹skill/Knowledge (religion)›, and ‹skill/Linguistics› (INT) to his list of class skills." note="Unlike most barbarians, a geminate invoker can be lawful, but must maintain a neutral element to his alignment. A geminate invoker who ceases to be neutral-aligned cannot activate his trance ability." alter="The barbarian's class skills~alignment"}

::ab[Trance (Ex)]{icon=boost-down free="A geminate invoker can enter a self-imposed trance, ignoring the physical limits of his body. This ability functions similarly to a barbarian's rage, except where noted below, and feats, spells, and abilities that affect a barbarian's rage (such as the ‹feat/Extra Rage› feat) apply to an geminate invoker's trance. Instead of the normal rage bonuses and penalties, a geminate invoker in a trance gains a +4 morale bonus to his Constitution; gains a +2 morale bonus on Reflex and Will saving throws; takes no penalties to AC; can still use Dexterity-, Intelligence-, and Charisma-based skills; and gains ‹feat/Diehard› as a bonus feat." special="The Constitution bonus increases to +6 and the save bonus increases to +3 when he gains *greater rage,* and they increase to a +8 Constitution bonus and a +4 bonus on saves when he gains *mighty rage.* A geminate invoker gains rage powers as per a normal barbarian, but he can never gain totem rage powers via this ability." alter=Rage}

::ab[Haunt Channeler (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A geminate invoker gains the power to sense and absorb lingering spiritual energy. This functions identically to the ‹class/medium› class ability of the same name, except the geminate invoker deals 1d6 points of damage to haunts for every 3 barbarian levels he has." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Spirit Conduit (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A geminate invoker can invite a spirit into his body each time he enters a trance, gaining a lesser ‹arc-barbarian/totem rage power› for the duration of his trance. He can select a new totem each time he enters his trance, though the GM might rule that certain totem spirits are simply not available in certain locations (such as channeling a celestial totem in an evil god's temple). Invoking a totem counter to his alignment (such as a neutral good character invoking a fiend totem) requires the geminate invoker to succeed at a DC 12 Charisma check each round to dominate the spirit. Failure indicates the spirit escapes, immediately ending the trance." imp8="The geminate invoker gains a second totem rage power, though it must use the previously granted *lesser totem rage power* as a prerequisite." imp12="He now also gains the *greater totem rage power* that uses the previously granted powers as prerequisites." replace="4th, 8th, and 12th level rage powers"}

giant_stalker.name: Giant Stalker
description: ## Giant Stalker
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/130›

Of the dangerous creatures that roam grim peaks and icy steppes, giants are among the most feared. They are cunning and capricious, and their ability to craft weapons and coordinate attacks makes them a constant threat to the nomadic tribes in the region. In the wilds where giants hunt, cleverness isn't the only thing the people need to survive - they require the fearlessness to confront a challenge, no matter the size.

Elders select the children of the tribe's fiercest barbarians for the honor of becoming giant stalkers. Through intense training, these children learn to spot and track giants using telltale signs along with more subtle clues (such as giants' unique scents). They learn the giants' speech and culture; they study their traditions and brutality; and most importantly, they foster their hatred of these oversized foes.

When ‹rule/Mammoth Lord<s› followings travel, giant stalkers scout ahead, securing safe passage through giant-infested territories. They clear giant hunting parties and ambushes, using gruesome trophies to mark the trails. Sometimes giant hunters send their own raiding parties into the mountains to capture young giants, as most followings keep giant thralls as symbols of status.

::ab[Harangue Giant (Ex)]{icon=power passive="While raging, a giant stalker can speak Giant. If the giant stalker already knows Giant, she gains a +2 bonus on Intimidate checks when speaking Giant while raging." alter=Rage}
::ab[Smell Giants (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A giant stalker gains the ‹umr/scent› special ability, but with regard only to humanoids with the giant subtype." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Giant Baiter (Su)]{l=3 icon=def move="A raging giant stalker can erupt into a violent or vulgar display directed at a single giant within 60 feet. This display is infused with secret magical traditions that draw upon the Mammoth Lords' ancestral enmities toward giants. A targeted giant must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the giant stalker's barbarian level + the giant stalker's Constitution modifier) or be compelled to focus its attacks on the giant stalker; this ability can also affect non-giant creatures that are Large or larger, though such creatures gain a +4 bonus on the Will save to resist the effect. This compulsion ends if the giant stalker is out of sight or inaccessible to the giant's attacks. While baited, a giant is distracted and enraged by the insults - a giant stalker gains a dodge bonus to her AC against any giant she has successfully baited. This dodge bonus is equal to one-third of her barbarian level. Once baited, a giant remains baited for a number of rounds equal to the giant stalker's Constitution modifier, or until the giant stalker's rage ends (whichever comes first). If the giant stalker baits a different giant, any previously baited giant is no longer baited." special="A giant that successfully saves against baiting is immune to that giant stalker's giant baiter ability for 24 hours. This is a language-dependent mind affecting effect." imp6="A giant stalker can bait two giants at once." imp9="Baiting giants becomes a @HLswift_action." imp12="She can bait up to three giants at once." imp15="Baiting giants becomes a @HLfree_action." imp18="A giant stalker can bait a number of giants equal to 3 + her Constitution modifier." replace="Trap sense"}
::ab[Giant Stalker Rage Powers]{icon=power head note="A giant stalker gains access to the following rage powers."}
::ab[Giant Stalker Defense (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="When raging, a giant stalker does not take a penalty to Armor Class against attacks from giants."}

::ab[Topple Giant (Ex)]{next icon=boost ability="When raging, the giant stalker does not provoke attacks of opportunity when she attempts to ‹rule/trip› a giant, and can attempt to trip a giant that is up to two size categories larger than she is."}

::ab[Underfoot (Ex)]{next icon=boost-lower move="When raging, the giant stalker can attempt to enter a giant's space, provided she is at least one size category smaller than the giant. Doing so requires a successful combat maneuver check against the giant's CMD. If she succeeds, she enters a square in the giant's space. If she makes an attack against the giant while sharing its space, the giant is treated as being ‹rule/flat-footed› against her. At the end of her turn, she exits the giant's space into any square adjacent to the giant's space." provokes=No}

hateful_rager.name: Hateful Rager
description: ## Hateful Rager
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/54›

From a young age, many half-orcs are treated cruelly, bullied, ridiculed, and made outcasts. While some hide their shame, others foster a deep, burning hatred that they channel into a raw fury and unleash against their enemies. These half-orcs are called hateful ragers. A hateful rager has the following class features.

::ab[Reduced Rage (Ex)]{icon=down note="At 2nd level and every level thereafter, a hateful rager only gains 1 additional round of rage per day instead of the normal 2 additional rounds of rage per day."}

::ab[Favored Enemy (Ex)]{icon=power-boost-down l=2 order=ca choice="A hateful rager selects a ‹ability/favored enemy›. This ability works identically to the ‹class/ranger› ability of the same name." ability="While raging, the hateful rager makes every effort to fight a favored enemy rather than other opponents. If aware of the presence of a favored enemy, the hateful rager must make a DC 20 Will save each round to attack another creature; failure means the barbarian must attack the favored enemy or move closer to that enemy. She may freely attack creatures preventing her from reaching that favored enemy (regardless of whether they are actively trying to prevent her attacks or merely in the way). She can avoid harmful obstacles normally in her attempts to reach the target and is not forced to take the shortest route." replace="2nd, 8th, 14th, and 20th level rage powers" imp8="The hateful rager selects a second favored enemy and increases her bonus against one favored enemy type, as described in the ranger class." imp14="The hateful rager selects a third favored enemy and increases her bonus against one favored enemy type." imp20="The hateful rager selects a fourth favored enemy and increases her bonus against one favored enemy type."}

::ab[Feed the Rage (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A hateful rager gains 1 additional round of rage for each favored enemy she knocks unconscious or kills in combat. These current rounds of rage can only be used to add to the duration of her rage, and disappear when the rage ends." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Amplified by Hate (Ex)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="A hateful rager adds ‹misc/half› her favored enemy bonus to the DC of any rage power she uses against a favored enemy." replace="Trap sense +3"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the hateful rager archetype: ‹ragepower/intimidating glare›, ‹ragepower/roused anger›, ‹ragepower/terrifying howl›, ‹ragepower/come and get me›, ‹ragepower/inspire ferocity›, ‹ragepower/overbearing advance›, ‹ragepower/reckless abandon›.

hurler.name: Hurler
description: ## Hurler
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/78›
A raging barbarian is frightening enough in melee, but some become skilled at throwing objects at their foes before closing in for the kill. A hurler has the following class feature.
::ab[Skilled Thrower (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="The hurler is skilled at throwing objects in combat. Increase the range increment of any thrown weapon or object by 10 feet." replace="Fast movement"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the hurler archetype: ‹ragepower/hurling›, ‹ragepower/greater hurling›, ‹ragepower/hurling charge›, ‹ragepower/lesser hurling›, ‹ragepower/strength surge›, ‹ragepower/surprise accuracy›.

invulnerable_rager.name: Invulnerable Rager
description: ## Invulnerable Rager
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/79›

Some barbarians learn to take whatever comes their way, shrugging off mortal wounds with ease. These barbarians invite their enemies to attack them, and use pain to fuel their rage. An invulnerable rager has the following class features.

::ab[Invulnerability (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="The invulnerable rager gains ‹rule/DR›/- equal to ‹misc/half› her barbarian level. This damage reduction is doubled against nonlethal damage." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge~damage reduction"}

::ab[Extreme Endurance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="The invulnerable rager is inured to either hot or cold climate effects (choose one) as if using ‹spell/endure elements›. In addition, the barbarian gains 1 point of fire or cold ‹umr/resistance› for every three levels beyond 3rd." replace="Trap sense"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the invulnerable rager archetype: ‹ragepower/come and get me›, ‹ragepower/guarded life›, ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/inspire ferocity›, ‹ragepower/reckless abandon›, ‹ragepower/renewed vigor›.

jungle_rager.name: Jungle Rager
description: ## Jungle Rager
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/39›

More "civilized" folk sometimes scoff at the people of the Mwangi Expanse, dismissing them as primitives with inferior technology. Although some tribes do rely on wood and bone over metal, their greatest advantage comes from mastery of the terrain. A jungle rager can disappear from sight, strike from hiding, and use the environment to shield herself from harm.

::ab[Home Ground Advantage (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="A jungle rager learns how to make optimal use of the natural cover of her homeland." choice="She chooses a specific terrain type from the ranger list of ‹ability/favored terrain«s›." order=cp passive="When in her chosen terrain, she increases her bonus to AC and on Reflex saving throws gained from ‹rule/cover›, partial cover, and improved cover (but not soft cover) by 1. In this terrain, she also increases the miss chance caused by ‹rule/concealment› for attacks against her by 5%." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Jungle Endurance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A jungle rager gains a bonus on Fortitude saves to resist the effects of ‹rule/hot weather› and diseases. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Home Ground Supremacy (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-def flavor="A jungle rager becomes even more capable when navigating and fighting in her preferred terrain. She gains the ‹ability/woodland stride› ranger ability, though this ability works only in the terrain she selected for the home ground advantage ability. In this terrain, she gains an additional +1 bonus to AC when benefiting from cover or improved cover (but not soft cover)." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Damage Reduction (Ex)]{icon=def special="A jungle rager gains this ability at 8th level, except her effective barbarian level for the ability is equal to her barbarian level - 1. In addition, this damage reduction is doubled against damage dealt by creatures with the swarm subtype. This ability otherwise functions as the barbarian ability of the same name." alter="Damage reduction"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the jungle rager archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/bestial climber›, ‹ragepower/hive totem›, ‹ragepower/internal fortitude›, ‹ragepower/low-light vision›, ‹ragepower/night vision›, ‹ragepower/raging climber›, ‹ragepower/scent›, ‹ragepower/sharpened accuracy›, ‹ragepower/surprise accuracy›.

mad_dog.name: Mad Dog
description: ## Mad Dog
‹SOURCE Animal Archive/7›
Though named for the wild savages who fight alongside rabid dogs, mad dogs employ all manner of beasts as their battle brethren.

::ab[War Beast (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A mad dog gains the service of an ‹ability/animal companion›, using the barbarian's level as her effective druid level." replace="2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th level rage powers"}

::ab[Pack Tactics (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A mad dog and her *war beast* gain a +4 bonus on attack rolls while flanking the same opponent (instead of the normal +2 bonus)." replace="Uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Rage (Ex)]{icon=down ability="A mad dog gains this ability at 4th level, except that her effective barbarian level for the ability is equal to her barbarian level - 3." alter=Rage}

::ab[Ferocious Fetch (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-melee ability="A mad dog's *war beast* gains ‹feat/Improved Drag› as a bonus feat. As a swift action, a mad dog can command her *war beast* to move toward an opponent within 30 feet and attempt a ‹rule/drag› combat maneuver to pull the target back toward the mad dog. The *war beast* can move before and after performing the maneuver, but this movement counts toward the *war beast's* total movement during its turn." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Damage Reduction (Ex)]{l=10 icon=def passive="A mad dog gains ‹rule/damage reduction› DR 1/-. A mad dog's *war beast* gains the same damage reduction as the mad dog." special="At 13th level and every 3 barbarian levels thereafter, this damage reduction rises by 1, to a maximum of DR 4/- at 19th level."}

::ab[Throat Cutter (Ex)]{l=14 icon=melee ability="Whenever her *war beast* succeeds at a bull rush, drag, grapple, overrun, or trip combat maneuver against an opponent threatened by the mad dog, a mad dog can take an attack of opportunity against that opponent." replace="Indomitable will"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the mad dog archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/beast totem›, ‹ragepower/bestial leaper›, ‹ragepower/ferocious beast›, ‹ragepower/greater ferocious beast›, ‹ragepower/primal scent›, ‹ragepower/raging leaper›, ‹ragepower/reckless abandon›, ‹ragepower/scent›, ‹ragepower/sprint›, ‹ragepower/swift foot›, ‹ragepower/terrifying howl›.

mooncursed.name: Mooncursed
description: ## Mooncursed
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/47›
Some barbarians exhibit an unusual form of lycanthropy powered by their fury. Such a character transforms willfully but can maintain her animal or hybrid forms only while raging.
::prereq{other="A mooncursed must either be a ‹type/humanoid› or ‹type/monstrous humanoid›."}

::ab[Shifting Rage (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-magic flavor="A mooncursed can call upon an animal totem or inner curse, transforming her into a savage beast." ability="Chooses ‹monster/bear›, ‹monster/boar›, ‹monster/crocodile›, ‹monster/shark›, ‹monster/tiger›, or ‹monster/wolf›. Once chosen, this animal can't be changed. When using rounds of her shifting rage, instead of the usual bonuses and penalties from rage, the mooncursed transforms into a Medium version of this animal's form as if under the influence of ‹spell/beast shape I›, treating her class level as her caster level whenever relevant. Other aspects of rage aside from the bonuses and penalties, such as the skill restrictions on skills and effects that require concentration, still apply during a shifting rage." alter=Rage}

::ab[Hybrid Rage (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost ability="A mooncursed can choose to assume a hybrid of her base type and alternate form instead of her usual alternate form during her shifting rage. If she does, the animal's forelimbs (or pectoral fins for the shark) remain hands able to manipulate objects and wield weapons as well as human hands do, she can walk on two legs (the shark's tail fins remain legs), and she can talk." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Greater Shifting Rage (Su)]{l=11 icon=boost ability="A mooncursed can assume larger alternate and hybrid forms. This functions like ‹spell/beast shape II›, and it allows the mooncursed to take on a Medium or Large version of her animal's form." replace="Greater rage"}

::ab[Mighty Shifting Rage (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="A mooncursed can assume even larger alternate and hybrid forms. This functions like ‹spell/beast shape III›, and it allows the mooncursed to take on a Medium, Large, or Huge version of her animal's form." replace="Mighty rage"}

mounted_fury.name: Mounted Fury
description: ## Mounted Fury
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/79›

Many barbarian tribes are masters of the horse, teaching their members how to ride from a young age. As a result, barbarians from such tribes are even more terrifying when mounted, using their steeds' speed and strength to great advantage. A mounted fury has the following class features.
::ab[Fast Rider (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="The speed of any mount the barbarian rides is increased by 10 feet." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Bestial Mount (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost ability="The mounted fury gains the service of a feral mount. This ability functions as a druid's ‹sidekick/animal companion›, using the barbarian's level - 4 as her effective druid level. This companion must be one that she is capable of riding and is suitable as a mount. A Medium barbarian can select a ‹companion/camel› or a ‹companion/horse›. A Small barbarian can select a ‹companion/pony› or a ‹companion/wolf›, but can also select a ‹companion/boar› or a ‹companion/dog› if she is at least 8th level. Whenever a barbarian is raging while mounted on her bestial mount, the mount gains a +2 morale bonus to its Strength." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the mounted fury archetype: ‹ragepower/ferocious mount›, ‹ragepower/ferocious trample›, ‹ragepower/greater ferocious mount›, ‹ragepower/greater ferocious trample›, ‹ragepower/spirit steed›.

numerian_liberator.name: Numerian Liberator
description: ## Numerian Liberator
‹SOURCE People of the River/6›

The Kellid barbarians that roam Numeria's badlands have a healthy mistrust of technology and its corruptive influence. Since the cataclysmic Rain of Stars millennia ago, these warriors have developed numerous techniques and skills to protect themselves from the strange mechanical monsters that roam Numeria. While many native Kellids oppose the Technic League and its espousal of foul alien artifacts, the most stalwart and staunchly dedicated of these technophobic traditionalists refer to themselves as Numerian liberators.
::cskill{gain=stl lose=climb}

::ab[Hard Hitter (Ex)]{l=2 flavor="A Numerian liberator can make devastating attacks against objects and foes with breakable components." icon=boost passive="Damage dealt by the barbarian's attacks ignores an amount of an object's or creature's hardness (if any) equal to ‹misc/half› her class level." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Disruptor]{l=5 icon=lower passive="For any foes in a Numerian liberator's threatened area, the DC to cast spells defensively increases by an amount equal to one-fifth her barbarian level." special="If the barbarian gains the ‹ragepower/disruptive› rage power, the two abilities stack." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Hide from Constructs (Su)]{l=14 icon=def flavor="A Numerian liberator can control her movement, breathing, body temperature, and other physiological signs to confuse the artificial visions of constructs." order=au ability="At will, the barbarian can make herself undetectable to constructs as if she were affected by a ‹spell/hide from undead› spell that instead hides the subject from constructs. Intelligent constructs receive a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 the barbarian's level + the barbarian's Constitution modifier) to see through this effect." usage="The barbarian must expend 1 round of rage for each round she uses this ability." replace="Indomitable will"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the Numerian liberator archetype: ‹ragepower/deadly accuracy›, ‹ragepower/energy absorption« (fire)›, ‹ragepower/energy resistance« (fire)›, ‹ragepower/greater energy resistance« (fire)›, ‹ragepower/gearbreaker›, ‹ragepower/guarded stance›, ‹ragepower/lethal accuracy›, ‹ragepower/low-light vision›, ‹ragepower/night vision›, ‹ragepower/rolling dodge›, ‹ragepower/reflexive dodge›, ‹ragepower/surprise accuracy›.

pack_hunter.name: Pack Hunter
description: ## Pack Hunter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/38›

Pack hunter barbarians team up to hunt dangerous prey to feed, to protect their camps, and as a rite of passage. Often, a tribe of pack hunters looks to a specific species of animal (such as wolves) who themselves hunt in packs, and view these animals as sacred manifestations of their people's traditions and values.

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power passive="Whenever a pack hunter would gain a new rage power, she can instead select a ‹main/teamwork feat›. She must still meet the prerequisites for the teamwork feat." alter="Rage power"}

::ab[In and Out (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A pack hunter gains a dodge bonus to AC against attacks of opportunity. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Sympathetic Rage (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost passive="If an ally within 30 feet is bloodraging or raging, a pack hunter counts as raging even if knocked unconscious. She has to have been raging when incapacitated to maintain her rage, and she ceases raging normally if she depletes her maximum number of rounds of rage in a day." replace="6th-level rage power"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the pack hunter archetype: ‹ragepower/contagious rage›, ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/no escape›, ‹ragepower/pack rage›, ‹ragepower/quick reflexes›, ‹ragepower/unexpected strike›.

pack_rager.name: Pack Rager
description: ## Pack Rager
‹SOURCE Monster Codex/92›

Barbarian rages can be a thing of savage beauty, exhibiting a lethal grace. While such uncontrolled displays of carnage often disregard group tactics, there are those barbarians whose rages inspire and spur on their allies during the ferocious dance of death.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power ability="The pack rager can take a bonus ‹main/teamwork feat›. This teamwork feat must also be a combat feat." repeat="The pack rager can take another bonus teamwork feat that is also a combat feat.~6~4" replace="2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th level rage powers"}

::ab[Raging Tactician]{l=7 icon=boost passive="While a pack rager is raging, she grants a single teamwork feat she knows to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear her. She chooses the feat at the start of the rage, and cannot change it during the rage. An ally who moves more than 30 feet away from the raging pack rager loses access to the feat, but regains it again each time he moves back within 30 feet of the raging pack rager. Allies do not need to meet the prerequisites of these teamwork feats." imp13="The pack rager chooses two teamwork feats when she enters a rage, and grants her allies the use of both of those feats when they are within 30 feet of her." imp16="The pack rager grants these teamwork feats as long as her allies are within 60 feet of her." imp19="The pack rager chooses three teamwork feats when she enters a rage, and grants her allies the use of all three feats when they are within 60 feet of her." replace="Damage reduction"}

primal_hunter.name: Primal Hunter
description: ## Primal Hunter
‹SOURCE Ranged Tactics Toolbox/11›

Rather than exploding with anger, primal hunters focus their rage to strike distant targets. Though able to hold their own in melee, primal hunters specialize in using some of the oldest of ranged weapons: bows, slings, and spears.

::ab[Exceptional Pull]{l=1 icon=power ability="A primal hunter gains ‹feat/Exceptional Pull› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the requirements." imp11="The strength rating of weapons she wields increases by 1 (weapons without a strength rating are not affected)." imp20="The strength rating now increases by 2." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Focused Rage (Ex)]{icon=ranged passive="While raging, a primal hunter gains a +2 bonus on attack rolls with ranged weapons. While raging, a primal hunter can attempt Stealth checks but doesn't gain a morale bonus on Will saves." imp11="The bonus becomes +3." imp20="The bonus becomes +4." alter=Rage}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the primal hunter archetype: ‹ragepower/deadly accuracy›, ‹ragepower/lethal accuracy›, ‹ragepower/reflexive dodge›, ‹ragepower/rolling dodge›, ‹ragepower/sharpened accuracy›, ‹ragepower/surprise accuracy›.

raging_cannibal.name: Raging Cannibal
description: ## Raging Cannibal
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/38;Champions of Corruption/27›

While savagery is not inherently evil, some barbaric cultures thrive on depravity and welcome the act of feeding on their own kind. The raging cannibal is a barbarian who feasts upon her fallen opponents not out of hunger, or even a taste for flesh, but rather because she believes consuming her foes and defiling their lost lives demonstrates her strength. This penchant for feasting on the flesh of her kin makes the raging cannibal an awkward companion to travel with for many groups, with rumors revolving around the raging cannibal's culinary exploits often causing tense situations that other party members might be called upon to resolve via diplomacy before the raging cannibal grows frustrated and decides to take matters into her own hands!
::ab[Animal Fury]{l=2 icon=power ability="A raging cannibal must select ‹ragepower/animal fury› as her first rage power." alter="The barbarian's 2nd-level rage power"}

::ab[Consume Vigor (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-melee ability="When a raging cannibal reduces a creature of the same creature type as herself to 0 or fewer hit points with her bite attack during combat while raging, she can consume a chunk of its body to gain a portion of its power. If the creature's CR equals or exceeds her barbarian level, the raging cannibal gains 1 additional round of rage. She can never consume the same creature's vigor more than once." imp5="The raging cannibal now gains 2 additional rounds of rage each time she uses this ability." imp8="She now gains 3 rounds of rage." imp11="She now gains 4 rounds of rage." imp14="She now gains 5 rounds of rage." imp17="She now gains 6 rounds of rage." imp20="She now gains 7 rounds of rage." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Intimidating Gouge (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-melee passive="When a raging cannibal confirms a critical hit against a creature of the same creature type as herself with her bite attack while raging, she gains a bonus on Intimidate checks equal to ‹misc/half› her barbarian level for the duration of the rage." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Feed from Fury (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-melee flavor="A raging cannibal is empowered by eating her enemies during combat." passive="When a raging cannibal confirms a critical hit against a creature of the same creature type as herself with her bite attack while raging, she gains a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her barbarian level. These temporary hit points stack with other temporary hit points gained from this ability but not with those from other sources. These temporary hit points fade after a period of time equal to 10 minutes per barbarian level." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Razor-Toothed Fury (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost-melee ability="When using her bite attack, the raging cannibal can take a -1 penalty on her attack roll to add 2 points of ‹rule/bleed› damage to the damage dealt." imp10="The penalty becomes -2 and the bleed damage becomes 4." imp14="The penalty becomes -3 and the bleed damage becomes 6." imp18="The penalty becomes -4 and the bleed damage becomes 8." replace="Damage reduction"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the raging cannibal archetype: ‹ragepower/internal fortitude›, ‹ragepower/intimidating glare›, ‹ragepower/primal scent›, ‹ragepower/scent›, ‹ragepower/terrifying howl›.

savage_barbarian.name: Savage Barbarian
description: ## Savage Barbarian
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/79›
Some barbarians are truly savage, having little training in modern arms. These savage barbarians learn to avoid blows and toughen up their skin. A savage barbarian has the following class features.

::ab[Naked Courage (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="The savage barbarian gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC and a +1 morale bonus on saving throws against fear when wearing no armor (shields are allowed)." increment="p!The bonuses~3~6" replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Natural Toughness (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def passive="The savage barbarian gains a +1 natural armor bonus to AC when wearing no armor (shields are allowed)." increment="The bonus~7~3" replace="Damage reduction"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the savage barbarian archetype: ‹ragepower/flesh wound›, ‹ragepower/guarded stance›, ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/intimidating glare›, ‹ragepower/rolling dodge›, ‹ragepower/superstition›, ‹ragepower/terrifying howl›.

savage_technologist.name: Savage Technologist
description: ## Savage Technologist
‹SOURCE Technology Guide/13›

Savage technologists exhort Kellids to rise up against the depredations of the Technic League. But rather than rejecting technology, they wield the League's own weapons against it. A savage technologist has the following class features.
::cskill{lose=kna~ha gain=diplo~kl~sm}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down note="A savage technologist is proficient in all firearms, but is not proficient with medium armor."}

::ab[Rage (Ex)]{icon=info note="A savage technologist can enter rage as a barbarian, except she gains a morale bonus to Strength and Dexterity instead of Strength and Constitution, and she does not take a penalty to Armor Class. She retains the bonus on Will saving throws. When a barbarian ability would increase the savage technologist's Strength while raging, it increases her Dexterity instead." alter=Rage}

::ab[Sword and Gun (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def-boost passive="When a raging savage technologist wields a one-handed firearm in one hand and a light or one-handed melee weapon in the other, she can make ranged attacks with the firearm without provoking attacks of opportunity. She also gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Two-Weapon Fighting› feat, but only if all attacks are made with those weapons." replace="Uncanny Dodge"}

::ab[Primal Magnetism (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A savage technologist adds her Strength modifier on Diplomacy checks when interacting with tribal cultures." order=pa ability="She can expend 2 rounds of rage (even if not raging) to add a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his barbarian level on a Diplomacy check."}

::ab[Crack Shot (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost-ranged passive="A savage technologist adds her Dexterity modifier to her damage rolls when making ranged attacks with a firearm while raging." replace="Improved Uncanny Dodge"}

scarred_rager.name: Scarred Rager
description: ## Scarred Rager
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/29›

Some barbarians wear marks of prowess and savagery upon their bodies. The scarred rager believes each wound tells the tales of her prowess and bravery. She augments these ragged trophies with brands and tattoos in order to win the notice and favor of her gods, ancestors, or totem spirits. The scarred rager's gnarled and exotic appearance terrifies civilized onlookers, but is awe-inspiring to her savage kin.

::ab[Terrifying Visage (Ex)]{icon=boost-lower passive="A scarred rager adds ‹misc/half› her barbarian level on Intimidate checks against humanoids who are not members of barbarian tribes. When dealing with barbarians, the scarred rager may choose to add this bonus on Diplomacy checks instead. The DC of any fear effect created by the scarred rager also increases by 1." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Tolerance (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A scarred rager who fails a save against an effect that causes her to become nauseated, sickened, fatigued, or exhausted can make a second save to negate the effect on the start of her next turn. Only one additional save is allowed. If the effect does not allow a saving throw, its duration is halved instead (minimum of 1 round)." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Scarification (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A scarred rager can ignore a number of points of bleed damage per round. This number is equal to one-third her barbarian level." imp15="A scarred rager can ignore 1 bleed effect each round." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Improved Tolerance (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="A scarred rager's *tolerance* ability also applies to effects that would cause her to become dazed, frightened, shaken, or stunned." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the scarred rager archetype: ‹ragepower/auspicious mark›, ‹ragepower/body bludgeon›, ‹ragepower/come and get me›, ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/inspire ferocity›, ‹ragepower/internal fortitude›, ‹ragepower/intimidating glare›, ‹ragepower/reckless abandon›, ‹ragepower/regenerative vigor›, ‹ragepower/renewed life›, ‹ragepower/renewed vigor›, ‹ragepower/renewed vitality›, ‹ragepower/roused anger›, ‹ragepower/terrifying howl›.

sea_reaver.name: Sea Reaver
description: ## Sea Reaver
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/29›

Not all barbarians hunt forests, plains, and mountains. Some are raiding terrors on the sea and coasts, pillaging those who hoard treasure and pursuing monsters of the deep. Some sea reavers are no more than hunters of the open sea, while others are raiders striking fear into coastal settlements within reach of the sea reavers' longships.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A sea reaver is not proficient with medium armor."}

::ab[Marine Terror (Ex)]{icon=power-boost-lower passive="A sea reaver can ‹misc/hold her breath› for a number of rounds equal to four times her Constitution score. In addition, a sea reaver can move normally though squares of standing water or bog that is 1 foot deep. It does not cost her extra movement to traverse these terrains. Lastly, a sea reaver ignores the normal ‹rule/cover› bonus to AC when attacking creatures that are partially immersed in water." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Eyes of the Storm (Ex)]{l=2 icon=lower-boost passive="A sea reaver ignores any ‹rule/concealment› provided by fog, rain, sleet, mist, wind, or other weather effects that is less than total concealment, and any penalties weather applies on Perception checks are halved." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Savage Sailor (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A sea reaver gains a bonus on Acrobatics, Climb, Profession (sailor), Survival, and Swim checks made in aquatic terrain, including aboard a ship or along shorelines. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-third› your barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Sure-Footed (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="A sea reaver takes no penalties when moving across slick surfaces, whether natural or magical (e.g., ‹spell/grease›, ‹spell/ice storm›, and ‹spell/sleet storm›). She is not at risk of falling, is not denied her Dexterity bonus when moving across such areas, and does not treat them as difficult terrain." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the sea reaver archetype: ‹ragepower/bestial leaper›, ‹ragepower/bestial swimmer›, ‹ragepower/come and get me›, ‹ragepower/hurling charge›, ‹ragepower/raging leaper›, ‹ragepower/raging swimmer›, ‹ragepower/rolling dodge›, ‹ragepower/smasher›.

sharptooth.name: Sharptooth
description: ## Sharptooth
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/39›

Coastal hunters and agents of the ocean's rage, sharptooth barbarians emulate the greatest predators of the sea. Sharks, in particular, are respected and admired by many such tribes, if not as manifestations of divine power, then merely as scions of the power and fury of the natural world.

::ab[Swim Like a Fish (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A sharptooth gains a swim speed of 10 feet." imp5="The swim speed becomes 15 feet." imp10="The swim speed becomes 20 feet." imp15="The swim speed becomes 25 feet." imp20="The swim speed becomes 30 feet." replace="Fast movement"}
::ab[Scent of Blood (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A sharptooth gains ‹umr/scent›." imp5="She also gains ‹umr/keen scent›." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Ocean Breath (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A sharptooth gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/one-third› her barbarian level. She adds this number to her Constitution score for the purposes of ‹misc/hold«ing» her breath›. She also adds it as a bonus on Constitution checks to continue holding her breath." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Blood in the Water (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost flavor="A sharptooth can tear the flesh from living creatures." passive="Her attacks add 3 points of ‹rule/bleed› damage while she is raging." imp12="This bleed damage increases to equal her Constitution modifier if it's higher." replace="6th- and 12th-level rage powers"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the sharptooth archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/bleeding blow›, ‹ragepower/contagious rage›, ‹ragepower/pack rage›, ‹ragepower/powerful blow›, ‹ragepower/primal scent›, ‹ragepower/raging swimmer›, ‹ragepower/scent›.

shoanti_burn_rider.name: Shoanti Burn Rider
description: ## Shoanti Burn Rider
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/44›

The Shoanti tribes who live in the Cinderlands have turned the deadly emberstorms that plague the region into a rite of passage. A few who survive the experience begin to embrace the adrenaline and surge of joy that comes from dancing along the edge of a whirling firestorm. These thrill-seekers are known as burn riders, and their exploits are celebrated by all Shoanti. Burn riders learn early on that speed and lightning-quick reactions are essential to avoid becoming trapped by the fickle whims of a raging emberstorm. They have an uncanny understanding of fire and can read the currents in the smoke.

::ab[Flame Runner (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost usage="Once per rage" flavor="A burn rider can goad her mount into a burst of incredible speed." ability="Her mount's speed increases by 10 feet until the end of the burn rider's turn." imp9="Her mount's speed now increases by 20 feet." imp15="Her mount's speed now increases by 30 feet." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Cinder Sight (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power passive="A Shoanti burn rider can see through fire, fog, and smoke without penalty as long as the light is sufficient to allow her to see normally." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Cinder Dance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-def immediate="When a burn rider succeeds at a Reflex save to avoid fire damage, she can move up to half her speed. If the burn rider ends this movement outside the area of effect, she takes no damage from the effect." special="After this sudden movement, the burn rider is ‹misc/staggered› until the end of her next turn. If the burn rider is mounted, her mount makes the movement instead, and both the mount and the burn rider are staggered until the end of the burn rider's next turn." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Mount]{l=4 icon=power ability="A burn rider gains a loyal mount. This ability functions like the druid's ‹sidekick/animal companion› ability, except that the burn rider always gains a ‹companion/horse› or ‹companion/pony› (though she can gain additional options at the GM's discretion) and her effective druid level is equal to her Shoanti burn rider level - 3." replace="4th, 8th, 12th, and 16th level rage powers"}

::ab[Give Me Fire (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost passive="If a Shoanti burn rider takes fire damage while raging, she regains 1 round of rage. The burn rider regains the round of rage only if the fire damage bypasses or exceeds any energy resistance or other protections in effect." special="The burn rider can only regain 1 round of rage per round." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the Shoanti burn rider archetype: ‹ragepower/elemental rage›, ‹ragepower/energy resistance›, ‹ragepower/ferocious mount›, ‹ragepower/ferocious trample›, ‹ragepower/increased damage reduction›, ‹ragepower/low-light vision›, ‹ragepower/night vision›, ‹ragepower/renewed vigor›, ‹ragepower/scent›, ‹ragepower/spirit steed›, ‹ragepower/sprint›, ‹ragepower/swift foot›.

superstitious.name: Superstitious
description: ## Superstitious
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/79›

Many barbarians distrust magic. While most just shy away from magic, others focus their rage on users of such foul arts. These barbarians are naturally distrusting, and develop keen senses to protect them from harm. A superstitious barbarian has the following class features.

::ab[Sixth Sense (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="The superstitious barbarian gains a bonus on initiative and an insight bonus to AC during surprise rounds. These bonuses are equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Keen Senses (Ex)]{icon=power l7="The superstitious barbarian gains ‹umr/low-light vision› (triple normal vision range in dim light if she already has low-light vision)." l10="She gains ‹umr/darkvision› 60 feet (or adds 60 feet to the range of any darkvision already possessed)." l13="She gains ‹umr/scent›." l16="She gains ‹umr/blindsense› 30 feet." l19="She gains ‹umr/blindsight› 30 feet." replace="Damage reduction"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the superstitious archetype: ‹ragepower/clear mind›, ‹ragepower/disruptive›, ‹ragepower/roused anger›, ‹ragepower/spellbreaker›, ‹ragepower/superstition›, ‹ragepower/witch hunter›.

titan_mauler.name: Titan Mauler
description: ## Titan Mauler
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/30›

In lands overrun by giants, dragons, and other hulking beasts, entire fellowships of barbarians hone tactics and traditions with one purpose - to bring low these massive foes. While her enemies' size makes the creatures strong, the titan mauler is even stronger, taking up weapons from her fallen foes that no lesser warrior can lift, and using them when she beseeches the spirits to grant her increased size and greater ferocity against her titanic foes.
::ab[Big Game Hunter (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="A titan mauler gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls and a +1 dodge bonus to AC in melee with creatures larger than themselves." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Jotungrip (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-down ability="A titan mauler may choose to wield a two-handed melee weapon in one hand with a -2 penalty on attack rolls while doing so. The weapon must be appropriately sized for her, and it is treated as one-handed when determining the effect of ‹feat/Power Attack›, Strength bonus to damage, and the like." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Massive Weapons (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-down ability="A titan mauler becomes skilled in the use of massive weapons looted from her titanic foes. She can use two-handed weapons meant for creatures one size category larger, but the penalty for doing so is increased by 4. However, the attack roll penalty for using weapons too large for her size is reduced by an amount equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level (to a minimum of 0)." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Evade Reach (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def swift="A titan mauler may choose one creature within her line of sight. Until the end of her turn, that target's reach is treated as if it were 5 feet shorter with respect to reaching the titan mauler." imp10="The target's reach is treated as 10 feet shorter." imp15="The target's reach is treated as 15 feet shorter." imp20="The target's reach is treated as 20 feet shorter." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Titanic Rage (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost-down ability="A titan mauler may choose to gain the benefits of ‹spell/enlarge person› when she enters a rage. While using *titanic rage,* she must spend 2 rounds of rage per round, and she becomes ‹misc/exhausted› rather than fatigued when the rage ends." replace="Indomitable will"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the titan mauler archetype: ‹ragepower/body bludgeon›, ‹ragepower/greater ground breaker›, ‹ragepower/ground breaker›, ‹ragepower/knockback›, ‹ragepower/mighty swing›, ‹ragepower/powerful blow›, ‹ragepower/smasher›, ‹ragepower/strength surge›.

targets: A barbarian often has a special totem that is the patron of her tribe. While individual totems vary, those in the tribe that call upon a totem receive similar abilities. Totem warrior barbarians can select from totem rage powers that match their totem.

@HL[Rage Powers:] The totem warrior is based entirely upon his totem rage powers. In addition to the totem powers themselves, the following rage powers complement the totem warrior archtype (depending on the totem chosen): ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/low-light vision›, ‹ragepower/night vision›, ‹ragepower/raging climber›, ‹ragepower/raging leaper›, ‹ragepower/raging swimmer›, and ‹ragepower/swift foot›.

Totem rage powers are only available to barbarians taking the ‹arc-barbarian/Totem Warrior› archetype. A barbarian cannot select from more than one group of totem rage powers; for example, a barbarian who selects a *beast* totem rage power cannot later choose to gain any of the *dragon* totem rage powers (any rage power with "dragon totem" in its title).

true_primitive.name: True Primitive
description: ## True Primitive
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/30›

Isolated and xenophobic tribes that dwell in areas untouched by civilization often see anything from cities and organized settlements as strange, dangerous, and decadent. They gain power from their truly primitive nature; their bodies and spirits are hardened by the wild and untainted existence far from the pathetic softness of so-called civilization. Even when forced to mingle with civilization, the true primitive stays apart in both traditions and trappings.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A true primitive is proficient with ‹eq-armor/hide armor› and armors made from ‹eq-material/bone›. A true primitive is also proficient with bone shields and the following weapons: ‹eq-weapon/battleaxe›, ‹eq-weapon/blowgun›, ‹eq-weapon/club›, ‹eq-weapon/greatclub›, ‹eq-weapon/handaxe›, ‹eq-weapon/longspear›, ‹eq-weapon/shortspear›, ‹eq-weapon/sling›, and ‹eq-weapon/spear›." alter="The barbarian's armor and weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Illiteracy]{icon=down note="A true primitive cannot read or write, and her superstition about such things leads her to refuse to ever learn to read or write, even if she multiclasses into other classes."}

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A true primitive has a ‹ability/favored terrain› representing her native homeland.
