This ability functions as the ranger class feature, and the true primitive's bonuses in that terrain improve by +2 at 5th level and every five levels thereafter. However, she does not gain any additional favored terrains." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Trophy Fetish (Ex)]{l=3 flavor="A true primitive collects teeth, bones, hair, and other trophies from vanquished enemies, representing their power and strength." icon=boost-def ability="A true primitive can attach a trophy fetish to one of the traditional true primitive weapons listed above. When wielding that weapon, the true primitive gains a +1 morale bonus on damage rolls. Furthermore, if that weapon has the ‹misc/fragile› property, once per day, the true primitive can ignore the effects of a single natural 1 roll. A trophy fetish can also be attached to a suit of hide or bone armor, granting the true primitive a +1 morale bonus on saving throws. Furthermore, if that armor has the fragile property, once per day, the true primitive can chose not to have the armor break on the confirmation of a critical hit made against her." imp8="A true primitive can use one additional trophy fetish. Multiple trophy fetishes can be attached to the same armor or weapon; their effects stack." imp13="She can now use three trophy fetishes." imp18="She can now use four trophy fetishes." special="A trophy fetish can be sundered (hardness 5, 1 hit point) but is not damaged by area attacks or attacks that do not specifically target it. It has no effect if attached to other types of weapons or armor." replace="Trap sense"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the true primitive archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/eater of magic›, ‹ragepower/ghost rager›, ‹ragepower/low-light vision›, ‹ragepower/night vision›, ‹ragepower/primal scent›, ‹ragepower/scent›, ‹ragepower/spell sunder›, ‹ragepower/sunder enchantment›, ‹ragepower/superstition›, ‹ragepower/witch hunter›.

untamed_rager.name: Untamed Rager
description: ## Untamed Rager
‹SOURCE Dirty Tactics Toolbox/15›
There are no rules in the wild. Some barbarians enter combat with only victory in mind and do anything in their power to achieve it.
::ab[Despicable Tactics]{l=2 icon=power ability="The untamed rager gains ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat." replace="Uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Feral Appearance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="The untamed rager gains a bonus on Intimidate checks equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}
::ab[Deplorable Tactics]{l=5 icon=power ability="The untamed rager gains ‹feat/Greater Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Dishonorable (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost-melee-def passive="The untamed rager gains a +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks when performing dirty tricks and to her CMD to resist others' dirty tricks." increment="The bonus~7~3" replace="Damage reduction"}

urban_barbarian.name: Urban Barbarian
description: ## Urban Barbarian
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/31›

Every barbarian knows that city life can soften the spirit and the body, but some barbarians take on the trappings and ways of their adoptive homes and bend their savage powers to its challenges. While these urban barbarians' rough edges are smoothed into civility, they can use their primal nature and upbringing to move with the ebb and flow of civilization's natural rhythms.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down passive="An urban barbarian is not proficient with medium armor."}
::cskill{lose=ha~kna~sur gain=diplo~kl~kno~ling~prof}

::ab[Crowd Control (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost-def passive="An urban barbarian gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls and a +1 dodge bonus to AC when adjacent to two or more enemies. In addition, her movement is not impeded by ‹rule/crowds›, and she gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› her barbarian level on Intimidate checks to influence crowds." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Controlled Rage (Ex)]{icon=boost-down ability="When an urban barbarian rages, instead of making a normal rage she applies a +4 morale bonus to her Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution. This bonus increases to +6 when she gains *greater rage* and +8 when she gains *mighty rage.* She may apply the full bonus to one ability score or may split the bonus between several scores in increments of +2. When using a controlled rage, an urban barbarian gains no bonus on Will saves, takes no penalties to AC, and can still use Intelligence-, Dexterity-, and Charisma-based skills. This ability otherwise follows the normal rules for rage." alter=Rage}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the urban barbarian archetype: ‹ragepower/boasting taunt›, ‹ragepower/clear mind›, ‹ragepower/deadly accuracy›, ‹ragepower/guarded stance›, ‹ragepower/intimidating glare›, ‹ragepower/lethal accuracy›, ‹ragepower/no escape›, ‹ragepower/quick reflexes›, ‹ragepower/perfect clarity›, ‹ragepower/sharpened accuracy›, ‹ragepower/surprise accuracy›, ‹ragepower/unexpected strike›.

wild_rager.name: Wild Rager
description: ## Wild Rager
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/31›

Rages are barely controlled, but there are those who wholly give in to their more savage side, letting their rages take them to a confusing and uncontrolled place of terrible savagery. These barbarians become beasts, consumed with absolute bloodlust to the point where they cannot tell friend from foe.

::ab[Uncontrolled Rage (Ex)]{icon=down passive="A wild rager's rage functions as normal, except that when she reduces a creature to 0 or fewer hit points, she must attempt a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the barbarian's level + the barbarian's Constitution modifier) or become *confused.* For the remainder of her current turn, she attacks the nearest creature other than herself. On the following round, refer to the ‹spell/confusion› spell to determine her actions. At the end of this round, and each round thereafter, she can attempt a new saving throw to end the confusion effect." special="The rounds during which she is confused do not count against the rounds she has spent raging that day, but she cannot end her rage voluntarily, nor can she use rage powers while confused."}

::ab[Wild Fighting (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-down flavor="Even when not raging, wild ragers often fight with reckless, savage abandon." ability="A wild rager using the ‹rule/full-attack› action can make one extra attack per round at her highest base attack bonus. Until the beginning of her next turn, however, she takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls and -4 penalty to AC." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Rage Conversion (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="A wild rager who fails a saving throw against any mind-affecting effect can attempt a new saving throw at the beginning of her next turn. If the save succeeds, that effect ends and she instead rages and becomes confused as noted above." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

@HL[Rage Powers:] The following rage powers complement the wild rager archetype: ‹ragepower/animal fury›, ‹ragepower/bleeding blow›, ‹ragepower/body bludgeon›, ‹ragepower/brawler›, ‹ragepower/crippling blow›, ‹ragepower/greater brawler›, ‹ragepower/intimidating glare›, ‹ragepower/mighty swing›, ‹ragepower/no escape›, ‹ragepower/powerful blow›, ‹ragepower/quick reflexes›.

wildborn.name: Wildborn
description: ## Wildborn
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/39›

Some barbarians are born or bred outside the reach of civilization. These wildborn fight and survive without ever seeing a forge or worked stone, and only dare a trip into the city when no other option is available.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power-warning passive="A wildborn is proficient with leather and hide armors (but not shields) and all simple weapons that can be crafted from bone, stone, or wood. If she uses any other kind of weapon or armor, she loses her abilities from this archetype (except illiteracy) for the next 24 hours." order=pa ability="A wildborn gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› and ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard› as bonus feats." alter="The barbarian's normal weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Illiteracy]{icon=down note="A wildborn cannot read or write. She can learn to read or write a language only if she takes a level in another class, and even then only by taking 1 or more ranks in Linguistics."}

::ab[Live Off the Land (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A wildborn adds ‹misc/half› her barbarian level as a bonus on Survival checks to hunt or gather food and gains a bonus on Fortitude saves to resist the effects of extreme temperatures or other environmental effects; the Fortitude save bonus is equal to one-third of her barbarian level." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power special="At 4th level, 10th level and 16th level, a wildborn can choose a bonus feat from the following rather than select a rage power: ‹feat/Alertness›, ‹feat/Animal Affinity›, ‹feat/Athletic›, ‹feat/Great Fortitude›, ‹feat/Improvised Weapon Mastery›, ‹feat/Iron Will›, ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes›, ‹feat/Self-Sufficient›, ‹feat/Stealthy›, or ‹feat/Throw Anything›." alter="Rage powers"}

::ab[Inexhaustible (Ex)]{icon=aid-def l=7 passive="A wildborn recovers nonlethal damage for each minute of rest as if an hour had passed. She applies twice her damage reduction gained from barbarian levels against nonlethal damage." alter="Damage reduction"}
